title: jwt.verify called with ignoreExpiration: true — expired tokens are accepted as valid

params.passDetail: jwt.verify sets ignoreExpiration to a value other than true — the `exp` claim is enforced and expired tokens are rejected.
params.failDetail: jwt.verify is called with ignoreExpiration: true, which disables the `exp` (expiry) check entirely. A leaked or stolen token is then valid forever — a session/credential that should have expired is silently accepted. Remove ignoreExpiration (it defaults to false) or set it to false.
params.absentCallDetail: Handler is in an auth/verify scope but does not call jwt.verify(); this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: jwt.verify does not pass an ignoreExpiration option literal; the default (false) enforces expiry. No action needed unless it is set elsewhere.

provenance.note: ignoreExpiration is documented as defaulting to false; setting it true accepts tokens past their `exp`. AI code generators sometimes add it to silence 'jwt expired' errors during development and leave it in production.
